description: Dotloop has been a regular part of my job for over 10 years. We use it for all real estate transaction docs, TREC forms, rental applications, leases, and so much more. It's great, and I much prefer it to other sites like Docusign.
author: Suzanne F.
pros: I've used Dotloop in my Director of Operations role for a real estate team since 2016. It's consistent, easy to use and manage, makes organizing and getting client signatures a breeze, and the support staff is so helpful for any issues or bugs that ever occur. It's worth the money, and reliable for all your real estate transaction needs!
cons: Some of the customization tools can be a tad confusing, namely when you switch real estate brokerages. My team has switched 4 times in ten years, and we've had some bumps with customizing new broker info in our account.

review_title: real estate contract management in dotloop
description: I have used that loop for the last three years straight and it’s easy to maneuver through the system and find the documents that I need as well as set up for online signatures
author: Tricia J.
pros: Dotloop platform is very easy to use for creating contracts from existing documents and for online signatures & document storage
cons: Dotloop does charge an annual fee but is provided through my brokerage complimentary to agents

review_title: Warning! Do not engage e-signing with DotLoop
description: Overall I will never suggest this product to any company ever. Their user contracts are not fair and put the user at a disadvantaged financially. If you need to go a different route due to finances, you will be unable to with this company!
author: Anna Lee W.
pros: We started using the Outlook because I was with a big box broker and it was their default e-signing system. We were already familiar with how to use it. So when we expanded to another state we started paying Ford out of pocket. It worked for us for a few years very well because we were familiar with it.
cons: This is a warning to any potential user of.DotLoop! You are tied into a contract for 12 months and whether you use the product or not you cannot escape paying out the end of your contract! There are no negotiations and no exceptions. Warning about that policy! Our brokerage used DotLoop for 5 years and always paid our bill on time. Florida association of Realtors offers a product that syncs better with the Florida forms. We are using that product now. It is a more user-friendly product through Form Simplicity I called DotLoop to cancel My subscription and was offered an appointment 2 weeks out to speak with a customer representative! I let them know that would not work for me and asked for a call back same day. I was asked what country and time zone I was in. I told them USA, eastern time. A phone call was scheduled 2 days out! That's ridiculous in a career where time is of the essence.

description: Good! I enjoy it to send documents to clients to get signatures and it right away comes back to me. It’s easier than going to the client
author: Dan P.
pros: You can send out documents to be digitally signed. It makes the process faster for clients instead of having hard copies
cons: I have used another system that is much easier and has way more functions. Dot loop does not have a forms piece

description: Overall been very please, almost never have issues
author: Russ C.
pros: Easy to upload docs and set for initials and signatures
cons: Some agents don't understand how it work or the fact that they can respond back to us using sent loop

review_title: Dotloop: A User-Friendly, Collaborative Transaction Management Tool
description: My overall experience with Dotloop is positive. I find it easy to use and highly collaborative, which streamlines transaction management and document handling. The platform's ability to facilitate real-time updates and interaction with other users makes your workflow more efficient. While there may be minor limitations or occasional hiccups, the benefits of its simplicity and collaborative features likely outweigh these, resulting in a productive and smooth experience.
author: Carla J.
pros: What I like most about Dotloop is its user-friendly interface and simplicity. The platform makes managing transactions straightforward, allowing for seamless document creation, editing, and signing. Additionally, it facilitates easy collaboration with other users, enabling real-time updates and ensuring everyone stays on the same page throughout the process. This combination of ease of use and collaborative features streamlines workflows and enhances productivity.
cons: Occasional glitches or slow performance, especially when working with large files. the platform’s interface, while generally user-friendly, could be more intuitive when navigating complex transactions. Additionally, i feel that certain advanced features are locked behind higher-tier plans, which may limit functionality for those using the basic version.

description: This is without a doubt the best document program available. I just wish it was mandatory that all real estate agents used it.
author: Scott D.
pros: Their customer service is the best I've ever experienced
cons: Its a big program, takes some getting used to.

description: We use this as our main doc signing platform
author: Jess T.
pros: WE love the ease of use and fillable templates
cons: Older users sometimes have issues with the functionality

description: Flawless experience thus far! I was introduced to this platform a few years ago and have been thrilled at how easy it is for my clients and myself!
author: Mai M.
pros: I love Dotloop! I was introduced to it by through real estate transactions and now use it in my own business transactions!
cons: NO cons. It is truly the most user friendly electronic doc signing I have used! I refer it to others for it exceptional value and ease of use!

description: it has worked very well and I have learned to by pass the modification issue by making my changes in a pdf writeable program 1st and then uploading to dotloop for signatures and initials only.
author: Jim C.
pros: can be used to have clients sign documents securely and quickly. best to use for those out of town or do not have access to a printer and scanner.
cons: difficult to modify documents once in the system. have to use dialogue boxes in order to modify once uploaded to the system

description: My experience with dot loop was. great. Besides the fact I could not access documents on multiple occasions due to viewing restrictions. It was frustrating as time as of the essence.
author: Josephine K.
pros: That is was very secure and privacy restrictions were heavily enforced.
cons: Hard to navigate and I experienced issues with not being able to access documents.

review_title: Dotloop user for over a decade
description: I haven’t encountered any inconvenience’s for myself or my clients on Dotloop this far. I’ve been a user for over a decade. The consistency has been outstanding.
author: Kristine M.
pros: Ease of use! It is easy to set up for my clients and the software makes it easy with clear prompts for my clients! It by far beats any other software I’ve tried.
cons: There isn’t anything I’ve disliked this far. I’m not very, “tech savvy,” but the folks at Dotloop have made it very easy for me to learn.

review_title: Generally Effective and Simple to Learn, But Pricey and Not Without Idiosyncrasies
description: At the end of the day, in real estate we just need something to keep us organized and keep up with the fast pace of modern transactions. Generally speaking, Dotloop allows this to happen via a simple user interface that most clients can figure out. There are probably more features to the software that I don't use (and perhaps, am not even aware of) but for my needs it's just fine. As I described previously, there are some things about it that make me want to tear my hair out, but having identified these annoyances, I'm usually able to avoid them and advise my clients on how to do the same. Would I switch to a different program if something better came along? Probably, in large part because Dotloop is more expensive than many of its competitors, and in the more than half-decade I've been using it, I've never seen the software updated to address some of the same problem areas that have been plaguing it all along. But as I said, it's 95% functional for my needs and I already know how to use it, which counts for a lot.
author: Andrew G.
pros: There are a lot of e-signature software programs out there right now. Many of these -- including Dotloop -- are pitched as specific to real estate transaction management, although there's no conceivable reason they couldn't be used in other contract-dependent industries as well. Having tried several of these programs, I've now been a consistent Dotloop user for at least five or six years, possibly longer. Part of that is simply because I've come to know the software and it's what I am comfortable using, which ultimately saves me time. But it really is straightforward, with an attractive user interface and great organizational capacity. I love being able to create folders within folders, to re-use Loops when clients return for multiple transactions, and to archive documents that are not being used. Basic functions like document splitting, renaming, saving, sharing and downloading are very easy and work as intended.
cons: As much as I like the software, it has a variety of quirks that cause me varying levels of irritation. I've learned a few tricks to keep the quirkiness to a minimum, but the program can still be truly obnoxious at times. One of my biggest pet peeves is when another agent wants to share documents with me from his/her Dotloop account; for instance, a buyer's agent sends me an offer for a property I have listed. If this buyer's agent shares it from her Loop (rather than just attaching a PDF to an email) Dotloop frequently creates a brand new Loop on my dashboard -- or worse, asks me to "sign up" for Dotloop because the agent has shared it to some variation of my email address that isn't technically my Dotloop address. So in a best case scenario my Loops become disorganized, and in a worst case scenario I can't even look at the offer unless I make a new account or ask the other agent to re-send. Many agents I know have this very same complaint, to the point where we have started adding comments in our MLS listings that say "Do NOT submit offers via Dotloop; email a PDF instead." Dotloop has some other quirks, including not saving signatures if two parties (say, husband and wife) are signing at the same time from their respective computers or mobile devices. I have to tell my clients "Make sure one of you signs completely and THEN the other one of you can do the same."

review_title: VERY USER FRIENDLY. WHAT YOU PUT INTO IT AND USE OUT OF IT WILL SHOW
description: I USE THIS WEEKLY WITH ALL MY CLIENTS AND THEIR REAL ESTATE TRANSACTIONS. I HAVE USED ANOTHER PRGRAM AND IT IS NOT AT ALL ORGANIZED AND HAS TOO MANY STEPS TO GET THE CONTRACT SIGNED AND THEN TO ADMIN FOR REVIEW. DOTLOOP MAKES THE WHOLE PROCESS SMOOTHER.
author: Maria C.
pros: THE FLOW OF THE ASSIGNED FIELDS ARE EASY FOR THE CLIENT TO SIGN. SAVES THE CLIENT TIME AND HAPPY TO USE IT. THE USER PUTS IN ALL THE INFO AT THE BEGINNING HELPS THE AUTO FILL TO HAVE EVERYTHING CORRECT SAVING TIME TO COMPLETE.
cons: WHEN SIGNEES ARE ABLE TO SIGN PASS THE PART THEY NEED TO SIGN. USUALLLY USER INOUT IS THE REASON OR SIGNEE IS CLICKING THE TAB INCORRECTLY. I DO NOT THINK IT IS DOTLOOP, I THINK IT IS HUMAN ERROR.

description: Dotloop provides a huge convenience for clients to be able to sign from their phone or computer anywhere they are. Gone are the days of having to drive across town just to get a signature or initial that may have been missed…not that I ever miss any…lol :0)
author: Ryan M.
pros: Dotloop is very widely used in my market so it makes it easy to transact on both sides of the deal with the other agent. I enjoy the features like document splitting and being able to customize the documents
cons: Nothing I can think of that I don’t like.

description: Many of my colleagues continue to use Dotloop even though our company is using a different software. It's because Dotloop is easier to use! I agree.
author: Barb S.
pros: The best thing about Dotloop is the end user experience. It is so easy to use.
cons: Nothing - I find it completely searchable, and easy to find my clients and their purchase/sale addresses.

review_title: Solid Solution for Reviewing and Signing Digital Documents
description: Only used a couple times, but it worked exactly as designed without any issues or glitches. I would definitely recommend.
author: Jordan M.
pros: Only used DotLoop maybe once or twice when a vendor sent me an agreement to sign. It worked seamlessly without any issues.
cons: No real cons. When I use a digital signing application, I just want it to work when I need to sign a document. This solution did just that.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great Product! & So simple to use
description: It made me feel secure when using it; my relator sent it to me for my purchase offer. The program would highlight for me where to sign, I just clicked on the highlighted area and my signature would be there or my initials. At the end of signing, I would receive a copy with my signature.
author: Kristal Y.
pros: It was simple and easy to use. It would not let me submit until each spot was filled out; this allowed me to take my time and review the documents attached.
cons: Nothing at all! Everything was very easy to use, and I cannot think of anything that I did not like.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great but not as well known
author: Ashley D.
pros: It’s easy to upload documents and prep them to get signatures.
cons: My clients hadn’t heard of dotloop as much as other esignature sites.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Tara H.
pros: Dotloop is easy to use and implement in everyday Real Estate needs. Managing forms and transaction history is simple.
cons: What I like least about dotloop is that you cannot merge loops if someone else has entered info. Also, sometimes fields will randomly delete when I am entering information.

review_title: Keeping me and my clients in the loop!
description: Of all the real estate technology, software, tools, apps available and there are thousands I’m sure, Dotloop is by far my most important tool as a real estate agent. When I first started my real estate career I literally had no idea what documents I needed to complete. My brokerage offered dotloop to all its agents. So I added my first loop, selected the option for residential seller, and voila—all the forms that I needed to have complete were right there in front of me. Had I not had dotloop, I would not have been able to complete that first deal on my own. I had never been shown what paperwork was needed. And it was so easy to use. Everything I needed was right there and so easy to use. I have since changed brokerages and dotloop made the swap super easy. I’m a bit of a tech geek and want to know all the features a software provides. So I always check for user manuals. And I loved that they offer a PDF manual. I later learned about the Certified Looper Course. I enrolled and completed the course and received my certification. The tips and tricks I learned in this course had saved me countless hours managing my transactions. I can’t say enough good things about this software. It’s my most important tool.
author: Jessica F.
pros: Most liked features include: Ease of use for user and their clients; secure; ability to add new interactive forms; export client contact information; integration with Zillow, Gmail, etc.; Co-Op docs; AUTOFILL;
cons: A feature that I would like to see added is within each loop you have a calendar tab that you can select. Once selected you see 3 full calendar months- the entire current months calendar, previous month and following month. You can toggle back to previous months or ahead to future months. The calendar shows all the key dates related to that loop both past and present. It pulls all the key dates from all the forms within the loop to give you a birds eye view of deadlines, expirations, etc. For example, a buyer loop would show the date offer was made, offer expiration date and time, offer accepted date, inspection period from start day to final day, anticipated closing date, financing contingency dates, extension periods, final walkthru date, Date of appraisal, Warranty start and end dates, etc. The system would provide you and your client with email and or text alerts for upcoming or expired dates. One click and you see a 3 month view of all the key dates for that loop.

description: I use dotloop as a single source for all of my document and file management and retention needs. The latest and greatest Forms, Contracts, Disclosures from my real estate associations always seem to be present. I love that I can even map interactive fields to my own internal/brokerage specific forms--fields that autofill based on the info used to create a loop. I have even created some forms that will do the math for me.
author: Candee W.
pros: Simple to use, highly customizable, very flexible yet consistent.
cons: The mobile app leaves a little to be desired--I get that maybe it can't be as robust as the desktop platform, but it is a little bit difficult to navigate, especially when trying to download/export a document to a device to be distributed via an email account. Sometimes it can be challenging to locate older, but still active, files.

description: Overall, Dotloop is a good experience and easy to use
author: Crystal M.
pros: easy to use, able to see the history, user-friendly
cons: it will decide to boot you at random times, causing you to have to get a code etc to log in, this is very inconvenient

author: Jim C.
pros: The learning curve is very easy. I like how I can have folders set up for every property and can archive items that I no longer need.
cons: I'd like to be able to remove a person from the loop

review_title: Fantastic Software Designed For The Real Estate Professional
description: I love using Dotloop with all my real estate transactions as it saves a tremendous amount of time with contract writing and delivery.
author: Bill I.
pros: The Dotloop software is easy to use and very intuitive which makes it very fast to learn and be proficient as using. Using Dotloop saves a tremendous amount of time and energy when writing or delivering contracts and the best part of it is that you can draft, sign and deliver contracts from the comfort of you own home or office. After using Dotloop, I can't imaging going back to the way things used to be.... Meeting clients to get signatures, taking contract to other Realtor, meeting other realtor after their clients sign contract and then delivering to my office... I'm already tired just typing the way it used to be. Thanks Dotloop for transforming the way contracts are done and saving Realtors many hours of time that we can spend with our family or time that can be put toward finding the next deal.
cons: The only thing that I would change about the software is that once you start a contract or form it cannot be deleted... It can be archived, but not deleted. It would be nice to be able to delete forms that were mistakenly added to the loop or started but not signed.
